core_mechanism: a periodic-orbit (semiclassical) correspondence in which isolated closed orbits of a dynamical system support a special non-ergodic subset of modes that evade generic mixing, producing an evenly-spaced (equidistant) ladder of spectral levels embedded in an otherwise ergodic spectrum
brief_reason: The abstract's whole method applies a periodic-orbit framework to locate and track closed orbits across the spectrum, tie them to a non-thermalising subset of eigenstates, and derive analytically the equidistant tower spacing of those modes that persists even after the underlying system is perturbed.
